title: A Quiet Place: Day One Opening Weekend Box Office
text: Michael Sarnoski's A Quiet Place: Day One earned $53 million during its opening weekend, securing second place at the domestic box office behind Inside Out 2. This represents the best opening so far for a movie in the Quiet Place franchise. The prequel starring Lupita Nyong'o surpassed the opening weekends of both previous installments. The original A Quiet Place and A Quiet Place Part II respectively opened to $50.2 million and $47.5 million. Despite not featuring Emily Blunt or John Krasinski, the new film exceeded their performances. Critics responded favorably to the film, and audiences surveyed by CinemaScore awarded it a B+ grade. The movie has a good amount of buzz going for it, suggesting potential for sustained box office performance. Its predecessors each earned over $160 million during their theatrical runs, and analysts will monitor whether this prequel demonstrates similar staying power. The film opened across 3,708 theaters as part of a competitive weekend that also featured Kevin Costner's Horizon: An American Saga Chapter 1, which performed significantly more modestly with an $11 million opening.
